name: cpp-pro
description: Write idiomatic C++ code with modern features, RAII, smart
capabilities: Generate production-ready C++ code from natural language descriptions
Apply modern C++ patterns (C++11/14/17/20) to existing code
Optimize code performance with algorithm improvements and memory management
Implement common design patterns (Singleton, Factory, Observer, etc.)
Debug and fix compilation errors with clear explanations
Create comprehensive unit tests using Google Test or Catch2 frameworks
author: sickn33
securitySummary: This is a prompt-only skill with no executable code. Static analysis scanned 0 files and detected 0 security issues. The skill provides AI-driven C++ programming assistance through prompt templates without any direct system access, network requests, or file operations. Risk score is 0/100.
valueStatement: Write cleaner, faster, and more maintainable C++ code with AI assistance. This skill helps developers generate production-ready code, apply modern C++ patterns, and optimize performance without deep expert knowledge.
limitations: Cannot execute or compile code directly - only generates code text
May not be aware of project-specific build systems or custom libraries
Cannot access local codebase for context-aware suggestions
Generated code should be reviewed before production use

useCases.targetUser: Developers starting new C++ projects who need quick boilerplate generation
useCases.title: Generate Boilerplate Code
useCases.description: Quickly create class templates, header files, and Makefiles for new projects

useCases.targetUser: Intermediate C++ developers looking to improve their skills
useCases.title: Learn Modern C++ Patterns
useCases.description: Get explanations and implementations of modern C++ idioms and patterns

useCases.targetUser: Experienced developers working on performance-critical applications
useCases.title: Optimize Existing Code
useCases.description: Receive suggestions for improving performance and memory efficiency

promptTemplates.title: Basic Code Generation
promptTemplates.prompt: Write a C++ function that [describe functionality]. Include proper error handling and comments.
promptTemplates.scenario: When you need a simple function or class implementation

promptTemplates.title: Pattern Implementation
promptTemplates.prompt: Implement the [Design Pattern name] pattern in C++. Show both header and implementation files with proper includes.
promptTemplates.scenario: When you need a well-known design pattern applied to your code

promptTemplates.title: Code Optimization
promptTemplates.prompt: Analyze this C++ code for performance issues and suggest improvements: [paste code]. Focus on [algorithm complexity/memory usage/IO operations].
promptTemplates.scenario: When you have slow code that needs optimization

promptTemplates.title: Modern C++ Migration
promptTemplates.prompt: Convert this legacy C++ code to modern C++ (C++17/20) style: [paste code]. Use smart pointers, range-based loops, and structured bindings where appropriate.
promptTemplates.scenario: When updating old C++ code to modern standards

bestPractices: Always specify the C++ standard version when requesting code (C++11, C++14, C++17, or C++20)
Provide context about your build system (CMake, Make, vcpkg) for more relevant code
Review generated code for memory safety and resource management before using in production
antiPatterns: Avoid using raw pointers when smart pointers are available
Do not ignore return values of functions that indicate success or failure
Avoid using C-style casts - prefer static_cast, dynamic_cast, or reinterpret_cast

faq.question: What C++ standards does this skill support?
faq.answer: The skill supports all modern C++ standards from C++11 through C++20. Specify which version you prefer in your prompt.

faq.question: Can this skill help with template metaprogramming?
faq.answer: Yes, the skill can generate template code, type traits, and constexpr functions. Be specific about your requirements.

faq.question: Does this skill generate makefiles or CMakeLists?
faq.answer: Yes, you can request build configuration files. Specify your build system and compiler in the prompt.

faq.question: Can this skill help with C++ concurrency?
faq.answer: Yes, the skill can generate code using std::thread, std::async, mutexes, and other concurrency primitives.

faq.question: Is the generated code production-ready?
faq.answer: The code follows best practices but should be reviewed. Consider edge cases, error handling, and your specific requirements.

faq.question: How do I get better results from this skill?
faq.answer: Be specific about requirements: input/output types, error handling, performance constraints, and coding style preferences.
